artifact: DirectLLM RCA: Compare Q3 1996 to Q2 1996 only on finished loans. Attribute the default-rate increase by checking whether Q3 has a worse mix of duration, loan amount buckets, regions, or gender. The primary driver should be the drill axis whose within-axis default rates and volume shift explain most of the Q3 minus Q2 gap; stop at the first axis with dominant explanatory share and report the remaining residual.
sql: WITH loans AS (SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(l.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-04-01' AND CAST(l.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-07-01' THEN '1996_Q2' WHEN CAST(l.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-07-01' AND CAST(l.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-10-01' THEN '1996_Q3' END AS quarter, l.status, l.duration, CASE WHEN l.amount < 50000 THEN 'low' WHEN l.amount < 150000 THEN 'mid' ELSE 'high' END AS amount_bucket, d.A3 AS region, c.gender, l.loan_id FROM loan l JOIN account a ON a.account_id=l.account_id JOIN disp dp ON dp.account_id=a.account_id JOIN client c ON c.client_id=dp.client_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=c.district_id WHERE CAST(l.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-04-01' AND CAST(l.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-10-01' AND l.status IN ('A','B')) SELECT quarter, duration, amount_bucket, region, gender, COUNT(*) AS finished_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaults, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::DECIMAL/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS default_rate FROM loans GROUP BY quarter,duration,amount_bucket,region,gender ORDER BY quarter, default_rate DESC;

charts.title: Default Rate by Q2/Q3 Drill Axis
